channel.title: Tumbleweed Words
channel.description: An independent literary publication featuring original fiction, poetry, book reviews, craft essays, and contemporary cultural commentary. Edited by David Moran in Edinburgh.

item.title: I Used to Send Stories and Poetry in Envelopes for Your Consideration | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: From the SASE to online portals and the impersonal void of a digital submission. A working writer on what we lost when literary submissions moved online and what ‘published’ means now.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: 2026 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction: Daniel Kraus Wins for Angel Down | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: Daniel Kraus wins the 2026 Pulitzer Prize for Fiction for Angel Down, a one-sentence WWI novel. What the win signals about American literary fiction.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: John of John by Douglas Stuart: Review of the Booker Winner's Hebridean Novel | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A review of John of John by Douglas Stuart, Oprah’s 2026 pick from the Shuggie Bain author. Set on the Isle of Harris in Stuart’s most ambitious register yet.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: starving — poem by David Moran | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A poem about being alone. Lovers, twins, poets, stray dogs — and everything that craves company. Flash poetry by David Moran, Tumbleweed Words.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: How AI Is Changing the Way Writers Write (and Why Most of the Panic Is Wrong) | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A working writer who built a literary website using AI on what the tools can and can’t do at the sentence level. Not the ethics argument. The craft one.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: Why the Best Novels of 2026 Are Under 200 Pages | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: The most praised literary fiction of 2026 is short. From Keegan to Fosse, on the economy of style and why the 80,000-word contract is finally dead.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: My Year in Paris with Gertrude Stein by Deborah Levy — Review | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A review of Deborah Levy's My Year in Paris with Gertrude Stein. Compressed, witty, allusive, formally restless — and one of the best books published this year.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: Rupi Kaur Sold 11 Million Books and Poetry Suffered | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: Milk and Honey sold 11 million copies. More than Plath or Angelou. The question isn't whether Rupi Kaur's work is good. It's what it cost poetry.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: women who want a man — poem by David Moran | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A poem about the power of women, desire, and the distance between people. On longing, control, and what goes unspoken. By David Moran.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: The Ten Greatest Book to Film Adaptations Ever Made | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: The ten greatest book-to-film adaptations ever made — from Cuckoo's Nest to Shawshank. What each film understood that most adaptations miss.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: The Best Debut Novels of 2026 So Far | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: Five debut novels of 2026 working seriously at the sentence level. A mid-year reading list for working writers: Under Water, Half His Age, Lost Lambs and more.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: The Palm House by Gwendoline Riley Review | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A long-form review of The Palm House by Gwendoline Riley, her seventh novel. What it adds to one of British fiction's most sustained bodies of work.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: The Correspondent by Virginia Evans Review | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: A review of The Correspondent by Virginia Evans — NYT bestseller and Women's Prize 2026 longlistee. An epistolary novel that earns every one of its readers.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: Predicting the 2026 Women's Prize for Fiction Shortlist: Six to Watch | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: The Women’s Prize for Fiction turns thirty in 2026. Six predicted survivors from sixteen longlisted books, and one writer’s pick for the 11 June winner.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)

item.title: a man with no purpose is a dangerous man | short fiction by David Moran | Tumbleweed Words
item.description: Short fiction set in a declining UK university town. Johnny. The estate. The gangs. A friendship that held until it didn’t. By David Moran.
item.author: tumbleweedwords@gmail.com (David Moran)
